chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجِبُ عَلَى مَنْ أَقَرَّ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ بِحَدٍّ وَ مَنْ لَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 34 – What Penalty (Hadd) is Obligated upon the one who accepts upon himself (confesses), and the one upon whom the Penalty (Hadd) is not Obligated

text: إِذَا أَقَرَّ الرَّجُلُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ بِحَدٍّ أَوْ فِرْيَةٍ ثُمَّ جَحَدَ جُلِدَ قُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ أَقَرَّ بِحَدٍّ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ يَبْلُغُ فِيهِ الرَّجْمَ أَ كُنْتَ تَرْجُمُهُ قَالَ لَا وَ لَكِنْ كُنْتُ ضَارِبَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘When the man accepts upon himself with a Penalty (Hadd), or fabrication, then denies it, would be whipped’. I said, ‘What is your view that if he accepts with a Penalty (Hadd) upon himself, in which stoning is reached, would youasws have him stoned?’ He asws said: ‘No. But, Iasws would have whipped him’.
